summary.intent: Address three P2 findings from Codex's round-6 review of PR #696 on the audit-agnostic-navigation-components branch of ars-ui.
summary.outcome: All three R6 findings were fixed, tested, committed, and pushed: the over-eager R5 controlled gate was reverted, concurrent lazy-load state preservation was corrected, and a visibility guard was added to RenameStart. 574 navigation tests pass, clippy clean, xci-fast green.

repo: The `controlled` split in the Steps component applies only to `step` navigation (gating `ctx.step.set()` in `step_change_plan`), NOT to `statuses` accounting — statuses are fully machine-owned and must always be updated regardless of whether `step` is controlled.

ChildrenLoaded previously reset the entire `load_state` map via `seed_load_state(&ctx.items)`, which would silently drop concurrent lazy-load deliveries after an ExpandAll fans N LoadChildren requests.
RenameStart, unlike FocusNode and DragStart, was missing the visibility guard that rejects nodes hidden under collapsed ancestors — allowing aria-activedescendant to reference DOM-absent ids.
The spec §1.5 post-completion contract requires the final step to be marked Complete regardless of step control mode.
Codex review rounds are numbered (R5, R6) and each finding within a round is tracked by thread id (e.g., #696-R6-T1).

code.finding: Removed the `!ctx.step.is_controlled()` guard around `statuses[current] = Complete`; updated `controlled_last_step_completion_*` test to assert statuses[3] == Complete after NextStep.

Replaced `ctx.load_state = seed_load_state(&ctx.items)` in ChildrenLoaded with a `seed_load_state_for_loaded` helper that seeds only the freshly-inserted subtree. Also added visibility guard to RenameStart mirroring FocusNode/DragStart pattern.
Added two new TDD tests: `concurrent_lazy_loads_preserve_other_loading_states_when_one_settles` (R6-T2) and `rename_start_rejected_for_node_hidden_under_collapsed_parent` (R6-T3).

workflow: Codex review findings are addressed round-by-round; each round may include pushback on fixes from the previous round (R6-T1 correctly reverted R5-T1).
The workflow is: fix → cargo fmt → cargo test → clippy -D warnings → spec validate → xci-fast → commit → push → reply on PR threads → re-trigger Codex.
xci-fast logs are written to /tmp and checked via sed after the background task completes.
Git commits for Codex review fixes include detailed per-finding breakdowns with thread references in the commit message body.

friction: R6-T1 required reverting a R5 fix that was itself introduced in response to a prior Codex finding — iterative review rounds can create back-and-forth churn when the scope of a fix is misapplied.
The `seed_load_state` full-map reset was a latent bug that only manifests under concurrent ExpandAll load scenarios, making it easy to miss without targeted TDD.
open_items: xci-fast was still running when the round-6 summary was written; results were confirmed only after the background task notification arrived.
Codex re-trigger is pending after push — round 7 findings not yet known.
PR #696 and issue #223 remain open pending full Codex sign-off.
